quality.rationale: 需多步仿射参数重标度消除加速度项与张量缩并推导，射影等价结构可推广至任意测地线轨迹重合的流形，题干与解答过程完整严密

origin.question:
Problem 8.26.
(b) Suppose $\mathbf{g}_{\mu \nu}$ and $\overline{\mathbf{g}}_{\mu \nu}$ have the same geodesics. Then show that
$$
\mathsf {S}_{\mu \nu}^{\lambda} = \delta_{\mu}^{\lambda} \Psi_{\nu} + \delta_{\nu}^{\lambda} \Psi_{\mu}
$$
where $\Psi_{\mu}$ are the components of a vector.
origin.solution:
(b) We cannot compare the geodesic equations of $\mathbf{g}$ and $\overline{\mathbf{g}}$ directly since they would involve different affine parameters $	au$ and $\overline{\tau}$ . If $\mathfrak{t}$ is an arbitrary function of $	au$ , a change of variable makes the geodesic equation
$$
\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathbf {x}^{\lambda}}{\mathrm {d t}^{2}} + \Gamma_{\mu \nu}^{\lambda} \frac {\mathrm {d x}^{\mu}}{\mathrm {d t}} \frac {\mathrm {d x}^{\nu}}{\mathrm {d t}} - \frac {\mathrm {d x}^{\lambda}}{\mathrm {d t}} \frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \tau}{\mathrm {d t}^{2}} \frac {\mathrm {d t}}{\mathrm {d} \tau} = 0 .
$$
To eliminate the $\mathrm{dr} / \mathrm{dt}$ term, multiply by $\mathrm{dx}^{\alpha} / \mathrm{dt}$ and antisymmetrize on $\alpha$ and $\lambda$ to get
$$
\mathfrak {u}^{\alpha} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathfrak {u}^{\lambda}}{\mathrm {d} t} - \mathfrak {u}^{\lambda} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathfrak {u}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d} t} + (\Gamma_{\mu \nu}^{\lambda} \mathfrak {u}^{\alpha} - \Gamma_{\mu \nu}^{\alpha} \mathfrak {u}^{\lambda}) \mathfrak {u}^{\mu} \mathfrak {u}^{\nu} = 0 ,
$$
where $\mathbf{u}^{\alpha}\equiv \mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\alpha} / \mathrm{dt}$ . If the world lines $\mathbf{u}^{\alpha}(\mathfrak{t})$ are geodesics for both $\mathbf{g}$ and $\overline{\mathbf{g}}$ then the above equation must also hold with $\Gamma$ replaced by $\overline{\Gamma}$ . Subtracting these two equations gives
$$
(\delta_{\sigma}^{a} \mathsf {S}_{\mu \nu}^{\lambda} - \delta_{\sigma}^{\lambda} \mathsf {S}_{\mu \nu}^{a}) \mathfrak {u}^{\sigma} \mathfrak {u}^{\mu} \mathfrak {u}^{\nu} = 0 ,
$$
which implies that
$$
\delta_{(\sigma}^{\alpha} \mathsf {S}_{\mu) \nu}^{\lambda} - \delta_{(\sigma}^{\lambda} \mathsf {S}_{\mu) \nu}^{\alpha} = 0 .
$$
When this equation is contracted on $\alpha$ and $
u$ the result is
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathsf {S}_{\sigma \mu}^{\lambda} = \delta_{\sigma}^{\lambda} \Phi_{\mu} + \delta_{\mu}^{\lambda} \Phi_{\sigma} \\ \Phi_{\alpha} \equiv \frac {1}{5} S_{\alpha \mu}^{\mu}. \\ \end{array}
$$
[See also L. P. Eisenhart, Riemannian Geometry (Princeton University Press, 1962), Section 40.]
